app.footer: 📖 App preparada para cargar muchos ejercicios desde exercises.json

multipleChoice.label: Multiple Choice
multipleChoice.description: Texto completo con todos los huecos numerados y una sección de respuestas por cada pregunta.

fillBlanks.label: Fill the Gaps
fillBlanks.description: Lectura completa con todos los huecos visibles y una hoja de respuestas para escribir una palabra.

wordFormation.label: Word Formation
wordFormation.description: Texto completo con huecos y palabra base indicada en cada caso, tal y como aparece en Cambridge.

sentenceTransformation.label: Sentence Transformation
sentenceTransformation.description: Las frases salen completas, con la palabra dada y un espacio para escribir solo el fragmento que falta.

exerciseSets.title: Exercise 1
exerciseSets.description: Bloque de ejemplo con los cuatro tipos de ejercicio.

multipleChoice.title: Studying black bears
multipleChoice.instructions: For questions 1 – 8, read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.
multipleChoice.note: Ahora ya no se muestra un trozo suelto del texto: ves todo el pasaje y debajo eliges una opción distinta para cada hueco.
multipleChoice.text:
After years studying North America’s black bears in the usual way, wildlife biologist Luke Robertson felt no closer to understanding the creatures. He realised that he had to {{1}} their trust. Abandoning scientific detachment, he took the daring step of forming relationships with the animals, bringing them food to gain their acceptance.

The {{2}} this has given him into their behaviour has allowed him to dispel certain myths about bears. {{3}} to popular belief, he contends that bears do not {{4}} as much for fruit as previously supposed. He also {{5}} claims that they are ferocious. He says that people should not be {{6}} by behaviour such as swatting paws on the ground, as this is a defensive, rather than an aggressive, act.

However, Robertson is no sentimentalist. After devoting years of his life to the bears, he is under no {{7}} about their feelings for him. It is clear that their interest in him does not {{8}} beyond the food he brings.
multipleChoice.explanation: En esta parte suelen decidir mucho las colocaciones: win trust, insight into, contrary to, care for, disputes claims, be misled, under no illusion, extend beyond.

fillBlanks.title: The origin of language
fillBlanks.instructions: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap.
fillBlanks.note: Aquí tienes el texto entero con todos los huecos visibles y una zona de respuesta independiente para escribir una sola palabra.
fillBlanks.text:
The truth is nobody really knows how language first began. Did we all start talking at around the same time {{9}} of the manner in which our brains had begun to develop?

Although there is a lack of clear evidence, people have come up with various theories about the origins of language. One recent theory is that human beings have evolved in {{10}} a way that we are programmed for language from the moment of birth. In {{11}} words, language came about as a result of an evolutionary change in our brains at some stage.

Language {{12}} well be programmed into the brain but, {{13}} this, people still need stimulus from others around them. From studies, we know that {{14}} children are isolated from human contact and have not learnt to construct sentences before they are ten, it is doubtful they will ever do so. This research shows, if {{15}} else, that language is a social activity, not something invented {{16}} isolation.
fillBlanks.explanation: En Open Cloze suelen caer conectores y expresiones fijas: because of, such a way, in other words, may well, despite this, if children..., if nothing else, in isolation.

wordFormation.title: Training sports champions
wordFormation.instructions: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap.
wordFormation.note: El texto aparece completo y en cada hueco se ve también la palabra base para que la respuesta se parezca más al formato real.
wordFormation.text:
What are the abilities that a professional athlete needs to guarantee that opponents can be {{17|COME}}? Clearly, speed, stamina and agility are essential, not to mention outstanding natural talent.

Both a rigorous and comprehensive {{18|FIT}} regime and a highly nutritious diet are vital for top-level performance. It is carbohydrates, rather than proteins and fat, that provide athletes with the {{19|ENDURE}} they need to compete. This means that pasta is more {{20|BENEFIT}} than eggs or meat.

Such a diet enables them to move very {{21|ENERGY}} when required. Regular training to increase muscular {{22|STRONG}} is also vital, and this is typically achieved through weight training. It is, of course, important to emphasize the role of {{23|DETERMINE}} in an athlete’s success, since training can be exhausting. Sports people are prone to injury but a quality training regime can ensure that the {{24|SEVERE}} of these can be minimized.
wordFormation.explanation: Aquí importa mucho reconocer la categoría gramatical necesaria: overcome (verbo), fitness / endurance / strength / determination / severity (sustantivos), beneficial (adjetivo) y energetically (adverbio).

sentenceTransformation.title: Key word transformations
sentenceTransformation.instructions: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given.
sentenceTransformation.note: En esta versión cada pregunta enseña la oración original, la palabra obligatoria y un hueco para escribir solo el fragmento que falta.

items.original: My brother now earns far less than he did when he was younger.
items.suffix: much now as he did when he was younger.

items.original: They are demolishing the old bus station and replacing it with a new one.
The number of students now at university has reached an all-time high, apparently.
items.prefix: The number of students now at university is

items.original: I’m disappointed with the Fishers’ new album when I compare it to their previous one.
items.prefix: I think the Fishers’ new album is

items.original: Anna got the job even though she didn’t have much experience in public relations.
‘I must warn you how dangerous it is to cycle at night without any lights,’ said the police officer to Max.
items.suffix: at night without any lights from the police officer.
items.answer: warning about the dangers of cycling

sentenceTransformation.explanation: En key word transformations conviene detectar la estructura equivalente exacta: nearly as much, being pulled down, the highest it has ever, in comparison with, in spite of, and warning about the dangers of cycling.
